2605.30039.title: Domain-Specific Data Synthesis for LLMs via Minimal Sufficient Representation Learning
2605.30039.paper_summary: Large Language Models have demonstrated remarkable progress in general-purpose capabilities and can achieve strong performance in specific domains through fine-tuning on domain-specific data. However, acquiring high-quality data for target domains remains a significant challenge. Existing data synthesis approaches follow a deductive paradigm, heavily relying on explicit domain descriptions expressed in natural language and careful prompt engineering, limiting their applicability in real-world scenarios where domains are difficult to describe or formally articulate. In this work, we tackle the underexplored problem of domain-specific data synthesis through an inductive paradigm, where the target domain is defined only through a set of reference examples, particularly when domain characteristics are difficult to articulate in natural language. We propose a novel framework, DOMINO, that learns a minimal sufficient domain representation from reference samples and leverages it to guide the generation of domain-aligned synthetic data. DOMINO integrates prompt tuning with a contrastive disentanglement objective to separate domain-level patterns from sample-specific noise, mitigating overfitting while preserving core domain characteristics. Theoretically, we prove that DOMINO expands the support of the synthetic data distribution, ensuring greater diversity. Empirically, on challenging coding benchmarks where domain definitions are implicit, fine-tuning on data synthesized by DOMINO improves Pass@1 accuracy by up to 4.63\% over strong, instruction-tuned backbones, demonstrating its effectiveness and robustness. This work establishes a new paradigm for domain-specific data synthesis, enabling practical and scalable domain adaptation without manual prompt design or natural language domain specifications.
2605.30039.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型在通用能力方面展現了顯著的進步，並且可以通過在特定領域的數據上進行微調來實現強大的性能。<lb/>然而，獲取目標領域的高質量數據仍然是一個重大挑戰。<lb/>現有的數據合成方法遵循演繹範式，嚴重依賴於用自然語言表達的明確領域描述和精心設計的提示，這限制了它們在現實世界場景中的適用性，特別是在領域難以描述或正式表達的情況下。<lb/>在這項工作中，我們著手解決通過歸納範式進行特定領域數據合成的未充分探索問題，其中目標領域僅通過一組參考示例來定義，特別是在領域特徵難以用自然語言表達的情況下。<lb/>我們提出了一個新穎的框架，DOMINO，該框架從參考樣本中學習最小的充分領域表示，並利用它來指導生成與領域對齊的合成數據。<lb/>DOMINO 將提示調整與對比解耦目標相結合，以將領域級模式與樣本特定噪聲分離，減輕過擬合的同時保留核心領域特徵。<lb/>理論上，我們證明了 DOMINO 擴展了合成數據分佈的支持，確保了更大的多樣性。<lb/>在挑戰性的編碼基準上，當領域定義是隱含的時候，對 DOMINO 合成的數據進行微調使 Pass@1 準確率提高了高達 4.63\%，超過強大的、經過指令調整的骨幹，展示了其有效性和穩健性。<lb/>這項工作建立了一個新的特定領域數據合成範式，使得在沒有手動提示設計或自然語言領域規範的情況下，實現實用且可擴展的領域適應成為可能。
2605.30039.author: Tong Ye et.al.
2605.30039.authors: Tong Ye, Hang Yu, Tengfei Ma, Xuhong Zhang, Jianguo Li, Peng Di, Peiyu Liu, Jianwei Yin, Wenhai Wang
